title: Network Traversal
module_title: Pivoting and Lateral Movement Through Network Segmentation
prerequisites: SSH tunnel and SOCKS proxy setup
learning_objectives: Establish pivot points through compromised web servers
Configure SSH tunnels and SOCKS proxies for internal access
Test credential reuse across network systems
Exploit internal hosts accessed through pivots
scenario: Shell on a web server between DMZ and internal network. Three internal hosts reachable only via pivot. Compromise internal systems.

scenario_context.setting: DMZ-located web server with network access to internal systems
scenario_context.challenge: Pivot through web server to compromise internal network
scenario_context.relevance: Common scenario; web servers often bridge DMZ and internal

subtasks.title: Pivot Setup
subtasks.description: SSH tunnel or SOCKS proxy through compromised host.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Configure SSH SOCKS proxy (-D) or local tunnel (-L) through compromised web server. Verify reachability to internal systems.

subtasks.title: Internal Scanning
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use nmap through SOCKS proxy (via proxychains). Enumerate internal hosts and services. Document accessible systems.
subtasks.evidence_required: Internal scan results showing accessible services

subtasks.title: Credential Reuse
subtasks.description: Test web server credentials on internal hosts.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Test SSH, SMB, RDP credentials obtained from web server against internal hosts. Document successful reuse patterns.

subtasks.title: Internal Host Compromise
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Exploit accessible internal systems through pivot. Establish shell access on 2+ internal hosts. Document exploitation path.

subtasks.title: Attack Chain Documentation
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Create visual diagram showing: external entry → web server → pivot → internal systems. Include network topology and access path narrative.

subtasks.title: Lateral Movement Report
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Write report with attack chain, systems compromised, data accessed, and specific recommendations for network segmentation.

resources.name: SSH Tunneling and Port Forwarding Guide

reflection_prompt: How did network segmentation affect your pivot strategy? What internal access would be invisible without pivoting?

quiz.question: What is the primary advantage of SSH SOCKS proxy (-D) over local port forwarding?
options: Allows scanning multiple internal hosts dynamically
quiz.explanation: SOCKS proxy (-D) creates a dynamic tunnel allowing flexible access to multiple internal systems, whereas -L forwards specific ports.

quiz.question: Why is credential reuse often successful during lateral movement?
options: Organizations use same credentials across systems
quiz.explanation: Many organizations reuse credentials across systems for convenience, making initial compromise highly effective for lateral movement.

portfolio_artifacts: Lateral movement report with segmentation recommendations
mentor_notes: Assess pivot technique selection and successful internal compromise. Attack chain should clearly show progression. 72-hour deadline accommodates multiple internal exploitations. Segmentation recommendations demonstrate strategic thinking.
